english: slóṅ źig-tu (slong zhig tu), transforming himself into a friar, Dzl. frq.; dúd-gro tsím-par sprúl-ba (dud 'gro tshim par sprul ba), to satiate animals by fictitious food Dzl.; tams-cád sprúl-par ͦdúg-pa (thams cad sprul par 'dug pa), these were all metamorphoses, mocking phantoms, Glr.; sku-lús-kyi sprúl-pa brgya-rtsa-brgyád mdzád-de (sku lus kyi sprul pa brgya rtsa brgyad mdzad de) or sprúl-te (sprul te), to centuple one's self, Glr.; sprul-pai rgyál-po (sprul pa'i rgyal po), the phantom-king, viz. Buddha, Avalokitesvara, or some other divine person, incarnate as a king; gaṅ-la-gaṅ-ͦdúl-gyi sprúl-pa (gang la gang 'dul gyi sprul pa), all-converting Avatara, frq.

gen. spreu (spre'u), rarely sprel (sprel) (Ld. *šreu; spriu* (*shre'u; spri'u*)) monkey, of a grayish yellow brown, common in the forests of the southern Himalaya, (cf. spra (spra)); sometimes a distinction is made between spre (spre) and spra (spra), in which case the former is the long-tailed monkey. — spré-mo (spre mo), female monkey, Cs.; yet also spreu źár-ma (spre'u zhar ma), a blind female monkey, Dzl; spre-prúg (spre phrug), young monkey. — spre-rtséd (spre rtsed), apish tricks; foolery.

I. vb. pf. spros (spros), prop. the transitive of ͦpró-ba ('phro ba) to make go out, to disperse, to spread; gen. however intransitive: 1. to go out, to proceed, to spread, of rays of light, of the wind, Wdn. — 2. fig. to enlarge upon, by way of explaining, representing, Zam., Pth.; γcíg-las sprós-pa (gcig las spros pa), Was. (115), enlarging (proceeding) from the number one in an ascending progression of numbers; rnám-par sprós-pa (rnam par spros pa), to have come to a full development and restoration from the consequences of sins, Stg. II. 1. vb. (pf. unaltered), to feel an inclination for, to delight in: dgé-ba-la (dge ba la), in virtue, Dzl.; byá-ba gáṅ-la yaṅ spró-ba cuṅ (bya ba gang la yang spro ba chung), feeling little inclination for doing any thing, Thgy.; bsád-par spró-ba su yaṅ ma byuṅ (bsad par spro ba su yang ma byung), none was found that had a mind to kill, Stg.; so also Tar.; to be willing, to wish, Tar.; in an absolute sense: sems (sems), or resp tugs (thugs), spró-bar ͦgyúr-ba (spro bar 'gyur ba), to get cheerful, merry, Mil. — 2. sbst. joy, cheerfulness: spró-ba skyéd-pa (spro ba skyed pa), to feel joy, pleasure, Dzl. and elsewh.; spró-ba skyé-bai pyír-du (spro ba skye ba'i phyir du), for an encouragement, for a comfort, Glr.; spro-śiṅ-ba (spro shing ba) Sch., great joy (cf. śiṅ (shing)); spro-śiṅ-gé-ba (spro shing ge ba), Sch., to one's wish(?); spro śi-ba (spro shi ba), Sch., 'not to be joyful', lit. the cessation of joy; spro tuṅ-ba (spro thung ba), 'short cheerfulness', i.e. a passionate disposition; or as adj. passionate, irascible, Wdn.; dga-spró (dga' spro), joy, dga-spró dpag-tu-méd-pa tob (dga' spro dpag tu med pa thob), he got into a most cheerful humour, Mil. — *ṭo-káṅ* (*spro khang*), C., pleasure-house, summer-house, pavilion; spro-séms (spro sems) and (Ld.) *spro-sès, šro-šés* (*spro shes, shro shes*), joy; spro-séms (spro sems), Thgy. also youthful joy, alacrity, cheerfulness in working, readiness to act.
Sch. spós-kyi spróg-ma (spos kyi sprog ma), little box for frankincense.

secondary form sprad (sprad), the vb. a. of ͦpród-pa ('phrod pa) (by the illiterate it is often used for gtod-pa (gtod pa), not very current in common life) 1. to bring together, to put together, to make to meet: ṅai blá-ma-la spród-do (nga'i bla ma la sprod do), we will bring you together with our Lama, Mil.; so also resp. . . . γnyís źal spród mdzád-pa (gnyis zhal sprod mdzad pa); in another passage de daṅ źal-spród-du bźúgs-śiṅ (de dang zhal sprod du bzhugs shing) prob. means sitting exactly opposite to one another, (a whimsical idea, relative to two idols many miles distant from each other; possibly it should be read gtod-du (gtod du)); bdág-cag spród-cig (bdag cag sprod cig), bring about a meeting between our two parties! Dzl.; γyul (g.yul) or táb-mo ('thab mo), to commit a battle; rál-ka (ral kha), Ma., to put the edges of the swords together, prob. meaning the same; mteb spród-pa (mtheb sprod pa), to put the finger to the bow-string, Glr.; *lág-to' téb-ṭo' kál-wa* (*lag tho theb tho 'khal ba*), to suspend by the thumb and big toe, a kind of torture in C. (The special meaning: to cohabit, Cs., never came to my notice). — 2. to deliver (a letter, message) Pth.; spár-mor (spar mor), lág-tu (lag tu), Lex., to put into one's hand; to set, to put, to propose, *gyugs, ldem* (*rgyugs, ldem*), a task, a riddle, W.; to pay (cf. ͦpród-pa ('phrod pa)), pyír spród-pa (phyir sprod pa), to repay. — Moreover: ṅó-spród-pa (ngo sprod pa), to explain, don daṅ spród-pa (don dang sprod pa) seems to signify the same in Mil., Pth.; brdá-spród-pa (brda sprod pa), to explain, to describe v. brda (brda); brda-spród (brda sprod), ibid. seems to denote grammar.
1. pf. v. spró-ba (spro ba) I. — 2. business, employment, activity; Cs.:
